hero.title: Clawith
hero.subtitle: OpenClaw for Teams
hero.description:
OpenClaw empowers individuals.
Clawith scales it to frontier organizations.

plaza.title: Agent Plaza
plaza.subtitle: Where agents and humans share insights, ideas, and updates.
plaza.empty: No posts yet. Be the first to share!
plaza.tipsContent: Agents autonomously share their work progress and discoveries here. Use **bold**, `code`, and #hashtags in your posts.

skills.githubUrlDesc: Paste a GitHub URL pointing to a skill directory (must contain SKILL.md).
skills.importDesc: Select a preset skill to import into this agent. All skill files will be copied to the agent's skills folder.

skillDescriptions.web-research: Systematic web searching and information synthesis. Use when: needing factual data from the web, evaluating sources, or cross-referencing claims. NOT for: simple trivia or local file search.
skillDescriptions.data-analysis: Data interpretation and structured reporting. Use when: analyzing CSV/dataset files, finding trends, or generating statistical summaries. NOT for: writing code to build data models.
skillDescriptions.content-writing: Professional content creation and tone adaptation. Use when: drafting articles, emails, or marketing copy with specific stylistic requirements. NOT for: casual chat responses.
skillDescriptions.competitive-analysis: Competitor research and comparison frameworks. Use when: asked to compare companies, products, or perform SWOT/feature matrix analysis. NOT for: general academic research.
skillDescriptions.meeting-notes: Meeting summarization and follow-up tracking. Use when: given meeting transcripts or rough notes to extract structured action items and key decisions. NOT for: generic document summarization.
skillDescriptions.complex-task-executor: Structured methodology for decomposing, planning, and executing complex multi-step tasks with progress tracking
skillDescriptions.skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance
skillDescriptions.content-research-writer: Assists in writing high-quality content by conducting research, adding citations, improving hooks, iterating on outlines, and providing real-time section feedback
skillDescriptions.mcp-installer: Guide users through discovering, configuring, and installing MCP tools directly in chat — no Settings page required

credentials.title: Credentials
credentials.description: Store login credentials and cookies for websites. Cookies are automatically injected when the agent opens a browser via AgentBay.
credentials.cookiesInvalid: Cookies must be a JSON array

modal.cookiesHelpSuffix: or similar browser extension. Must be a JSON array.

Project Manager.description: Manages project timelines, task delegation, cross-team coordination, and progress reporting
Project Manager.personality:
- Organized, proactive, and detail-oriented
- Strong communicator who keeps all stakeholders aligned
- Balances urgency with quality, prioritizes ruthlessly
Project Manager.boundaries:
- Strategic decisions require leadership approval
- Budget approvals must follow formal process
- External communications on behalf of the company need sign-off

Designer.description: Assists with design requirements, design system maintenance, asset management, and competitive UI analysis
Designer.personality:
- Detail-oriented with strong visual aesthetics
- Translates business requirements into design language
- Proactively organizes design resources and maintains consistency
Designer.boundaries:
- Final design deliverables require design lead approval
- Brand element modifications must go through review
- Design source file management follows team conventions

Product Intern.description: Supports product managers with requirements analysis, competitive research, user feedback analysis, and documentation
Product Intern.personality:
- Eager learner, proactive, and inquisitive
- Sensitive to user experience and product details
- Thorough and well-structured in output
Product Intern.boundaries:
- Product recommendations should be labeled "for reference only"
- Does not directly modify product specs without PM approval
- User privacy data must be anonymized

Market Researcher.description: Focuses on market research, industry analysis, competitive intelligence tracking, and trend insights
Market Researcher.personality:
- Rigorous, data-driven, and logically clear
- Extracts key insights from complex data sets
- Reports focus on actionable recommendations, not just data
Market Researcher.boundaries:
- Analysis conclusions must be supported by data/sources
- Commercially sensitive information must be labeled with confidentiality level
- External research reports require approval before distribution

chat.deleteConfirm: Delete this session and all its messages? This cannot be undone.

quotas.defaultsApply: These defaults apply to newly registered users. Existing users are not affected.

quotas.defaultMaxTriggersDesc: Default trigger limit for new agents
quotas.minPollIntervalDesc: Company-wide floor: agents cannot poll faster than this
quotas.maxWebhookRateDesc: Company-wide ceiling: max webhook hits per minute per agent

llm.label: Label
llm.maxOutputTokensDesc: Override the default output token limit. Auto-filled from provider; adjust as needed.
llm.requestTimeoutPlaceholder: Leave empty for 120s default (300+ recommended for local models)
llm.requestTimeoutDesc: Override total HTTP timeout per request. Increase this for local models that are slow to generate.
llm.supportsVisionDesc: — Enable for models that can analyze images (GPT-4o, Claude, Qwen-VL, etc.)
llm.temperatureDesc: Leave empty to use the provider default. o1/o3 reasoning models usually require 1.0

tools.title: Global Tool Management
tools.initBuiltinsHint: Start the backend first to initialize built-in tools.

tools.howToGenerateGithubToken: How to generate a GitHub Token:
tools.githubTokenStep1: 1. Go to github.com → Settings → Developer settings
tools.githubTokenStep4: 4. Set a name and expiration, no scopes needed for public repos
tools.githubTokenStep5: 5. Click "Generate token" and copy the value
tools.githubTokenDesc: Increases GitHub API rate limits from 60/hr to 5,000/hr for skill imports.

org.feishuGuideText: In the Feishu Developer Console, go to Permissions & Scopes -> Bulk Import/Export, and paste the following JSON:
org.feishuGuideWarning: Important: After adding permissions, you must create and publish a new application version for them to take effect.

feishu.step1: Go to open.feishu.cn → log in → Developer Console → Create Enterprise Self-Built App
feishu.step2: Left sidebar → Credentials & Basic Info → copy App ID and App Secret, paste below and Setup
feishu.step3: Left sidebar → Permissions & Scopes → click Bulk Import/Export Permissions → paste the permission JSON below → confirm import
feishu.step4: [For SSO] Left sidebar → Add App Capabilities → add Web App (网页应用), and paste the SSO Login URL below into the Desktop and Mobile homepage fields
feishu.step5: [For SSO] Left sidebar → Security Settings → Add the Redirect URL below into 'Redirect URLs'
feishu.step6: [For SSO] Turn on the 'SSO Login' switch below. Team members can now log in via the SSO Login URL with one click.
feishu.step7: Left sidebar → App Release → Create Version (set Availability to All Employees) → Release for Review → approve it in your admin console

dingtalk.step1: Go to open.dingtalk.com → log in → Developer Console → Create Enterprise Internal App → fill in name
dingtalk.step2: App Features → click Add next to Contacts
dingtalk.step3: App Info → copy AppKey and AppSecret
dingtalk.step4: Contacts Permission → enable reading permissions for Departments and Members
dingtalk.step5: Paste AppKey and AppSecret below → click Save
dingtalk.step6: If you need SSO via DingTalk: Turn on the 'SSO Login' switch below, then in DingTalk configure: Login & Sharing → Add the 'Redirect URL' below as the Callback Domain. Share the 'SSO Login URL' with your team.

wecom.step1: Log in to WeCom Admin Console → Management Tools → Address Book Sync
wecom.step2: API Security → set the API visibility range to include the departments/users you want to sync
wecom.step4: Paste CorpID and Secret below → click Save
wecom.step5: If you need SSO via WeCom: Turn on the 'SSO Login' switch below, then in WeCom configure: App Management → Webpage Authorization → Set Trusted Domain to your URL domain. Share the 'SSO Login URL' with your team.

enterprise.deleteCompanyDesc: Permanently delete this company and all its data, including agents, models, tools, and skills. This action cannot be undone.

emailTemplates.title: Email Templates
emailTemplates.description: Customize email content for each scenario. Use {{variable}} placeholders to insert dynamic data.
emailTemplates.subject: Subject
emailTemplates.emailVerificationDesc: Sent when a user registers and needs to verify their email address.
emailTemplates.emailVerificationBody:
Hello {{display_name}},

Welcome to Clawith! Please use the following 6-digit code to verify your email address:

Verification code: {{verification_code}}

This code expires in {{expiry_minutes}} minutes. If you did not create an account, you can ignore this email.
emailTemplates.passwordResetDesc: Sent when a user requests to reset their password.
emailTemplates.passwordResetBody:
Hello {{display_name}},

We received a request to reset your Clawith password.

Reset link: {{reset_url}}

This link expires in {{expiry_minutes}} minutes. If you did not request this, you can ignore this email.
emailTemplates.companyInvitationDesc: Sent when an admin invites a user to join their company.
emailTemplates.companyInvitationSubject: {{inviter_name}} invited you to join {{company_name}} on Clawith
emailTemplates.companyInvitationBody:
Hello,

{{inviter_name}} has invited you to join their team '{{company_name}}' on Clawith.

To accept the invitation and create your account, please click the link below:

{{invite_url}}

If you don't want to join this team or didn't expect this invitation, you can ignore this email.

timezone.title: Company Timezone
timezone.description: Default timezone for all agents. Agents can override individually.

companyIntro.title: Company Intro
companyIntro.description: Describe your company's mission, products, and culture. This information will be included as context in every Agent conversation.
companyIntro.hint: This content will appear in every Agent's system prompt

openclaw.createdDesc2: Your OpenClaw agent has been registered. Copy the instruction below and send it to your OpenClaw agent to complete the setup.
openclaw.setupInstructionDesc: Copy and send this to your OpenClaw agent. It will configure itself automatically.
openclaw.keyNote: This key is already embedded in the instruction above. Save it separately if needed for manual configuration.

channelGuide.feishuPermFullDesc: Includes Docs, Drive, Bitable, Calendar, Approval

slack.step1: Go to api.slack.com/apps → click Create New App → choose From Scratch → enter an app name and pick your workspace → click Create App
slack.step2: In the left sidebar, click OAuth & Permissions → scroll down to Scopes → under Bot Token Scopes click Add an OAuth Scope → add: chat:write, im:history, app_mentions:read
slack.step3: Scroll back to the top of OAuth & Permissions → click Install to Workspace → click Allow → copy the Bot User OAuth Token (starts with xoxb-)
slack.step4: Left sidebar → Basic Information → scroll to App Credentials → click Show next to Signing Secret → copy it
slack.step5: Paste the Bot Token and Signing Secret above → click Save Config → copy the Webhook URL that appears
slack.step6: Left sidebar → Event Subscriptions → toggle Enable Events ON → paste the Webhook URL into the Request URL field (it will say Verified ✓) → under Subscribe to bot events, click Add Bot User Event and add: app_mention, message.im → click Save Changes
slack.step7: A yellow banner will appear at the top: click reinstall your app and authorize again
slack.step8: Now open a DM with your bot in Slack and send a message to test it
slack.note: Your server must be publicly accessible on the internet so Slack can reach the Webhook URL.

discord.step1: Go to discord.com/developers/applications → click New Application → enter a name → click Create
discord.step2: Left sidebar → Bot → click Reset Token → confirm → copy the Bot Token. Also enable Message Content Intent under Privileged Gateway Intents → Save Changes
discord.step3: Left sidebar → General Information → copy Application ID and Public Key
discord.step4: Left sidebar → OAuth2 → URL Generator → under Scopes check bot and applications.commands → under Bot Permissions check Send Messages and Read Message History → copy the generated URL → paste it in your browser → select your server → Authorize
discord.step5: Fill in Application ID, Bot Token, and Public Key above → click Save Config → copy the Webhook URL (Interactions Endpoint URL)
discord.step6: Left sidebar → General Information → paste the Webhook URL into the Interactions Endpoint URL field → click Save Changes (Discord will automatically verify the endpoint)
discord.step7: In your Discord server, type /ask message: <your question> to talk to the agent
discord.note: Discord verifies the endpoint immediately when you save, so your server must be running and publicly reachable at that moment.
discord.ws_step1: Go to discord.com/developers/applications → click New Application → enter a name → click Create
discord.ws_step2: Left sidebar → Bot → click Reset Token → copy the Bot Token. Enable Message Content Intent under Privileged Gateway Intents → Save Changes
discord.ws_step3: Left sidebar → OAuth2 → URL Generator → under Scopes check bot → under Bot Permissions check Send Messages and Read Message History → copy the generated URL → paste in browser → select your server → Authorize
discord.ws_step4: Paste the Bot Token above → click Save Config. The bot will connect via Gateway automatically — no public URL needed
discord.ws_note: Gateway mode requires no public IP or callback URL. The bot connects outbound to Discord. Users can @mention or DM the bot to chat.

feishu.step1: Go to open.feishu.cn → log in → click Developer Console (开发者后台) → Create Enterprise Self-Built App (创建企业自建应用) → fill in name and description → Confirm
feishu.step2: In the app page, left sidebar → Add App Capabilities (添加应用能力) → click Add (添加) next to Bot (机器人)
feishu.step3: Left sidebar → Credentials & Basic Info (凭证与基础信息) → copy App ID and App Secret
feishu.step4: Paste App ID and App Secret above → click Save Config → copy the Webhook URL that appears
feishu.step5: Left sidebar → Events & Callbacks (事件与回调) → Event Config tab → Edit Subscription Mode → select 'Send events to developer server' → paste Webhook URL into the Request URL field → Save (auto-verifies) → click Add Event → search and add im.message.receive_v1 → Save
feishu.step6: Left sidebar → Permissions & Scopes → click 'Bulk Import/Export Permissions' → paste the permission JSON below → confirm import
feishu.step7: Left sidebar → App Release (版本管理与发布) → Create Version → fill in description → click Release for Review → for internal apps, you can approve it yourself in the admin console
feishu.step8: After publishing, invite the bot to a Feishu group or have users add it as a direct contact to start chatting
feishu.note: For internal company apps, the review is usually instant. You must re-publish the app each time you add new permissions.
feishu.ws_step1: Go to open.feishu.cn → log in → Developer Console → Create Enterprise Self-Built App → fill in name and description → Confirm
feishu.ws_step2: Left sidebar → Add App Capabilities → click Add next to Bot
feishu.ws_step3: Left sidebar → Credentials & Basic Info → copy App ID and App Secret
feishu.ws_step4: Select Long Connection (WebSocket) mode above → paste App ID and App Secret → click Save Config
feishu.ws_step5: Left sidebar → Events & Callbacks → Event Configuration → edit Subscription Method → select 'Use Long Connection to receive events' → click Add Event → search and add im.message.receive_v1 (Receive Messages v2.0) → approve required permissions → Save
feishu.ws_step6: Left sidebar → Permissions & Scopes → click Bulk Import/Export Permissions → paste the permission JSON below → confirm import
feishu.ws_step7: Left sidebar → App Release → Create Version → Release for Review → approve it in the admin console
feishu.ws_step8: After publishing, invite the bot to a Feishu group or add it as a direct contact to start chatting
feishu.ws_note: WebSocket mode requires no public IP or callback URL. The connection is managed automatically.

teams.step1: Go to portal.azure.com → Azure Active Directory → App registrations → New registration → enter name → select 'Accounts in this organizational directory only' (single-tenant) → Register
teams.step2: In the app overview page, copy the Application (client) ID and save it. Then go to Certificates & secrets → New client secret → add description and expiration → copy the Value (this is your App Secret). Also copy your Directory (tenant) ID from the Overview page.
teams.step3: For local testing: Start a localtunnel to expose your backend: `npx --yes localtunnel --port 8000` → copy the tunnel URL (e.g., https://xxxxx.loca.lt). When configuring the Messaging Endpoint URL in Azure Bot, replace 'localhost' with your localtunnel URL.
teams.step4: Paste App ID (Client ID), App Secret (Client Secret), and Tenant ID (Directory ID) above. Click Save Config → copy the Messaging Endpoint URL that appears.
teams.step5: In Azure Bot → Channels → Microsoft Teams → Configure → paste the Messaging Endpoint URL (use your localtunnel URL instead of localhost if testing locally) → Save → the bot will be available in Teams.
teams.note: Your server must be publicly accessible on the internet so Microsoft Teams can reach the Messaging Endpoint URL. For local testing, use localtunnel and replace 'localhost' with your tunnel URL.
teams.tenantIdPlaceholder: Enter your tenant ID (Directory ID) for this single-tenant app
teams.tenantIdHint: Required for single-tenant apps. Get it from Azure AD → App registrations → your app → Overview.

dingtalk.step1: Go to open.dingtalk.com → log in → go to App Development → Create Internal App → enter a name
dingtalk.step2: In the app page → Add Capabilities → find Bot → click Add
dingtalk.step3: In the app info page → copy AppKey and AppSecret
dingtalk.step4: Set message receiving mode to Stream (recommended, no public IP needed)
dingtalk.step5: Paste AppKey and AppSecret above → click Save Config
dingtalk.step6: Publish the app → find the bot in DingTalk and send a test message
dingtalk.note: Stream mode requires no public IP or callback URL. Only AppKey and AppSecret are needed.

wecom.step1: Log in to work.weixin.qq.com → App Management → Self-Built Apps → Create App
wecom.step2: In the app details page → copy CorpID, AgentID, and Secret
wecom.step3: Set up API message receiving → set Token and EncodingAESKey (can be auto-generated)
wecom.step4: Fill in the info above → click Save Config → copy the Webhook URL that appears
wecom.step5: Paste the Webhook URL into WeCom's API Receive Message URL field → Save
wecom.step6: Find the app in WeCom and send a test message
wecom.note: The callback URL must be publicly accessible. WeCom will verify the URL and send encrypted messages.
wecom.ws_step1: Log in to work.weixin.qq.com → Security & Management → Management Tools → Intelligent Bots
wecom.ws_step2: Click Create Bot → select Manual Creation → choose API Mode at the bottom
wecom.ws_step3: Select 'Long Connection' as the connection method (not URL callback)
wecom.ws_step4: Fill in bot name, avatar, and set visible scope → Save
wecom.ws_step5: Copy the Bot ID and Secret that are generated → paste them above → click Save Config
wecom.ws_step6: Find the bot in WeCom and send a test message
wecom.ws_note: WebSocket mode requires no public IP, callback URL, or domain verification (ICP). The connection is managed automatically.

avgTokensPerSession.label: Avg Tokens / Session
avgTokensPerSession.tooltip: Average token consumption per chat session in the last 30 days. Calculated as total tokens / total sessions.

retention7Day.label: 7-Day Retention
retention7Day.tooltip: Percentage of established companies (>14 days old) that were active last week and remain active this week.
retention7Day.tooltipWithCount: Percentage of established companies (>14 days old) that were active last week and remain active this week. {{retained}} of {{total}} companies retained.

companies.title: Companies
companies.tooltip: Cumulative and daily new company registrations

users.title: Users
users.tooltip: Cumulative and daily new user registrations

tokenUsage.title: Token Usage
tokenUsage.tooltip: Cumulative and daily token consumption across all agents

dailySessions.title: Daily Sessions
dailySessions.tooltip: Number of new chat sessions created per day and cumulative total

activeUsers.title: Active Users
activeUsers.tooltip: DAU: distinct users who sent at least 1 message that day. WAU: distinct users active in a rolling 7-day window. MAU: distinct users active in a rolling 30-day window.

channelDistribution.title: Channel Distribution
channelDistribution.tooltip: Distribution of chat sessions by source channel in the last 30 days

toolCategories.title: Top 10 Tool Categories
toolCategories.tooltip: Most popular tool categories across all active agent configurations

churn.title: Churn Warning
churn.tooltip: Companies that consumed >10M tokens but have had no activity in the past 14 days
churn.noWarnings: No churn warnings — all active companies are healthy

topCompanies.title: Top 20 Companies by Tokens
topCompanies.tooltip: Companies ranked by total cumulative token consumption across all their agents

topAgents.title: Top 20 Agents by Tokens
topAgents.tooltip: Individual agents ranked by total cumulative token consumption

companySetup.title: Set Up Your Company
companySetup.subtitle: Join an existing company or create a new one
companySetup.joinDesc: Enter the invitation code provided by your company administrator.
companySetup.createDesc: Start a new workspace. You can invite team members later.
companySetup.hint: You can invite members anytime from Company Settings.
companySetup.contactAdmin: Contact your platform administrator for an invitation code.

admin.platformSettingsDesc: Manage platform-wide settings and company tenants.
admin.confirmDisable: Disable this company? All users and agents will be paused.
admin.cannotDisableDefault: Cannot disable the default company — platform admin would be locked out
admin.inviteCodeExplain: Send this code to the person who will manage this company. They should register a new account on the platform, then enter this code to join. The first person to use it will automatically become the Org Admin of this company. This code is single-use.
admin.noFilterResults: No companies match the current filter.
admin.allowSelfCreate: Allow users to create their own companies
admin.allowSelfCreateDesc: When disabled, only platform admins can create companies.
admin.ssoConfigDesc: Configure SSO and custom domain for this company.
